steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: For coordinate-circle questions, read the center and radius from the equation or graph the circle as a check. For diagram facts, mark equal radii, diameters, tangents, and arcs before doing algebra.

steps.title: Identify the circle's center and radius
steps.content: Compare $(x+5)^2 + (y-14)^2 = 144$ to the standard form $(x-h)^2 + (y-k)^2 = r^2$. Center: $(-5,\ 14)$. Radius: $\sqrt{144} = 12$.

steps.title: Find the range of valid $x$-values on the circle
steps.content: The $x$-coordinate of any point on the circle lies within one radius of the center's $x$-coordinate: $$-5 - 12 \le a \le -5 + 12$$ $$-17 \le a \le 7$$

steps.title: Test each choice against the range
steps.content:
• A) $a = -18$: $-18 \lt -17$, outside the range — eliminate.
  • B) $a = -12$: $-17 \le -12 \le 7$, inside the range — valid.
  • C) $a = 12$: $12 \gt 7$, outside the range — eliminate.
  • D) $a = 14$: $14 \gt 7$, outside the range — eliminate.
That matches Choice B — -12.
